claims.claim: A valid claimant receives an accepted GrantDrop receipt only after a Stellar testnet manageData transaction exists.
claims.user_action: Use the testnet signer, keep the valid input selected, click Claim grant, then inspect the receipt.
claims.visible_result: The receipt card shows ACCEPTED, public inputs, a Stellar transaction hash, and a Stellar Expert link.
claims.backend_or_storage_proof: .hunter/accepted-claim.report.json pass=true; .hunter/stellar-tx-verification.json successful=true; tx 098a940b40a92f15657571d33456b6110a669503cbfd4d08d13f3d74f0b13c9a is on Horizon testnet.
claims.test: .hunter/accepted-claim.report.json includes a receipt id, public inputs, Stellar Expert URL, and matching successful Horizon tx verification.

claims.claim: The eligibility decision is gated by a Groth16 proof and local verification key, with public signals exposed for inspection.
claims.user_action: Run the proof verification command or inspect an accepted receipt panel.
claims.visible_result: The inspection panel shows protocol groth16, public inputs, proof digest, and verification asset paths.
claims.backend_or_storage_proof: public/proofs/verification_key.json, public/proofs/grantdrop.wasm, public/proofs/grantdrop_final.zkey, and .hunter/proof-verification.txt with snarkJS OK.
claims.test: npm run proof:verify returns snarkJS OK and accepted browser run shows public inputs in .hunter/accepted-claim.report.json.

claims.claim: A receipt route can be opened by a separate browser context without a signer, while missing receipt ids show a recovery path.
claims.user_action: Open the copied accepted receipt URL from a second context, then open /receipts/not-yet-created for the recovery path.
claims.visible_result: The shared receipt URL rebuilds an ACCEPTED read-only receipt, and a missing id shows Receipt not found with Return to campaign.
claims.backend_or_storage_proof: .hunter/accepted-claim.report.json second_context.readonly=true; .hunter/runtime-interaction.report.json pass=true with second_context=true and reload_assertion=true; receipt storage is IndexedDB-backed in src/services/receiptStore.ts.
claims.proof_type: Playwright runtime report + shared receipt payload
claims.test: runtime case 'second context can open public receipt replay route' passed in Chromium, Firefox, WebKit, and Pixel 7.

claims.claim: The stored receipt keeps the nullifier, wallet, status, proof digest, and Stellar reference, not the private eligibility fact.
judge_surface: SUBMISSION.md: Why it fits Stellar Hacks ZK
video narration: Why it is real
claims.user_action: Inspect an accepted receipt and compare its fields with the receipt storage type.
claims.visible_result: The receipt shows nullifier and public inputs, while the stored Receipt type has no secret or eligibility-fact field.
claims.backend_or_storage_proof: src/types.ts Receipt fields; src/services/receiptStore.ts saveReceipt; .hunter/accepted-claim.report.json contains nullifier/public inputs/tx and no private secret.
claims.proof_type: source-level storage boundary + browser receipt artifact
claims.test: accepted claim report differs from witness input: it records receipt, nullifier, publicInputs, and tx link without the secret field.
